title: Emotions
description: Vocabulary for describing feelings and emotional states.

entries.definition: Feeling pleased or glad.
entries.example: I felt happy after passing the exam.

entries.definition: Feeling nervous because something bad might happen.
entries.example: I was worried about the interview.

entries.definition: Feeling very happy and enthusiastic about something.
entries.example: I felt excited about starting my new course.

entries.definition: Unable to understand something clearly.
entries.example: I was confused by the instructions.

entries.definition: Feeling pleased because of something you have done.
entries.example: She felt proud of her progress.

entries.definition: Unhappy because you are alone or feel separate from others.
entries.example: It can feel lonely when you move to a new city.

entries.definition: Relaxed and not worried or angry.
entries.example: Try to stay calm before the test.

entries.definition: Feeling uncomfortable because of something awkward.
entries.example: She felt embarrassed when she forgot his name.

entries.definition: Annoyed because you cannot do or change something.
entries.example: He felt frustrated when the app did not work.

entries.definition: Happy because something difficult or worrying has ended.
entries.example: I felt relieved when I passed the exam.

entries.definition: Worried or afraid about something that may happen.
entries.example: I felt nervous before speaking in class.

entries.definition: Feeling sure about yourself or your ability.
entries.example: She became more confident after practising English every day.

entries.definition: Sad because something was not as good as expected.
entries.example: I was disappointed when the lesson was cancelled.

entries.definition: Feeling something unexpected has happened.
entries.example: I was surprised by how friendly everyone was.

entries.definition: Feeling tired because something is not interesting.
entries.example: The students were bored during the long wait.

entries.definition: Wanting to know or learn more about something.
entries.example: I was curious about life in Cambridge.

entries.definition: Feeling thankful for something kind or helpful.
entries.example: I was grateful for my teacher's support.

entries.definition: Worried and under pressure.
entries.example: I felt stressed before the deadline.

entries.definition: Feeling that something good may happen.
entries.example: She felt hopeful about finding a new job.

entries.definition: Unhappy, worried or disappointed.
entries.example: He was upset after receiving bad news.
